title: Resistivity
short: A material's intrinsic opposition to current, ρ, measured in ohm-metres (Ω·m).
full:
Resistivity ρ is a property of the material itself, independent of shape: it says how strongly a given substance resists current. Copper has a low resistivity (a good conductor); rubber's is enormous (an insulator).

To get the resistance of an actual wire you fold in its geometry: R = ρ·L/S, longer and thinner being more resistive. Resistivity also rises with temperature in metals.

The SI unit is the ohm-metre (Ω·m).
